response:
Section 1  
I need to write a Python function that counts the number of occurrences of a specific word in a given text, ignoring case sensitivity. The function should take two inputs: `text` (a string) and `word` (a string). The function should return an integer representing how many times the specified word appears in the text.

Section 2  
To handle case insensitivity, I will convert both the `text` and `word` to lowercase (or uppercase) before comparing them. This ensures that words like "Freedom" and "freedom" are treated as the same word.

Section 3  
Next, I will split the `text` into words using the `split()` method. This will allow me to process each word individually. I will then iterate through the list of words and count how many times the `word` (in lowercase) appears.

Section 4  
Finally, I will return the count as an integer. I will also include a postscript that specifies the requirements for the response.

P.S.: Your response must have 4 sections. Mark the beginning of each section with Section X, such as:
Section 1
[content of section 1]
Section 2
[content of section 2] No two adjacent words can start with consecutive letters of the alphabet.
